notes: v6.9 successfully built a real executable test harness (replacing the v6.8 scaffolding stub).
Solana test validator (2.1.20) boots in ~3s with deployed KLend BPF (klend.so, 10 MB) loaded.
Payer airdrop succeeds. Initial setup tx for token+treasury+mint succeeds.
initLendingMarket ix rejected with AnchorError::InstructionFallbackNotFound (0x65) for BOTH the raw-name sighash (sha256(name)[:8]) AND the global: prefix sighash (sha256('global:'+name)[:8]).

Attempts to determine the deployed-BPF discriminator scheme via static-binary-pattern-search failed: 0 matches for any variant of every instruction name. This is consistent with anchor-lang 0.29+ runtime sighash computation (not statically embedded) — but the runtime computation ultimately still rejects our wire signature.

Verdict: cannot drive the deployed kamino BPF from this host using direct signature-on-the-wire construction without first rebuilding klend locally with an anchor-lang version that produces the matching discriminator. Options for v6.10+: (a) anchor build of klend against anchor 0.31 + 1.81 toolchain — but klend pins 0.29/1.74 — likely fails toolchain friction; (b) write a simplified klend-mirror test program in anchor 0.31 with just the flash-loan surface and target *that*; (c) pin down kamino's actual deployed anchor-lang version by source-reviewing git tags of the klend repo between May 2024 and the deployment date.

next_session.preferred_path: (b) anchor 0.31 test program mirroring initLendingMarket + initReserve + flashBorrowReserveLiquidity + flashRepayReserveLiquidity; validated against the v6.9 harness without further rewrite.
next_session.alternative_path: (a) rebuild kamino from source; only if (b) is judged insufficient for substrate coverage
